description: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability exists in V8's WebAssembly tier-up logic inside Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper (src/runtime/runtime-wasm.cc). When a Wasm module uses the generic wasm-to-js wrapper and an indirect function call triggers tier-up, the runtime iterates over all WasmDispatchTables to locate the entry matching the given WasmApiFunctionRef. It accesses table->ref(entry_index) on every table using the same entry_index without first verifying that entry_index is within the table's bounds. Because individual dispatch tables can have different sizes, a small table processed before the correct (larger) table will be accessed at an index that exceeds its length(), triggering a fatal DCHECK failure (index < length()) in debug builds and an out-of-bounds memory read in release builds. The vulnerability is exploitable by constructing a Wasm module with at least two tables of different sizes (e.g., size 1 and size 3), placing the target import in the larger table at an index beyond the smaller table's length, and invoking the function enough times to trigger tier-up. The fix is to add a bounds check (entry_index < table->length()) before calling table->ref(entry_index) at both affected sites in Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper.

fixes.evidence_text: Please merge [https://crrev.com/c/5309898](<https://crrev.com/c/5309898>) to M123 Beta by EOD Tuesday, 27 February, and M122 Stable by EOD, Thursday, 29 February so this fix can be included in their next respective updates -- thank you and apologies for all the email pings that would have resulted from all my tag changes.
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/5309898 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
